about.title: About Us
about.body:
The Žižkov Tunnel Association is an independent initiative founded in 2025 by prominent figures from the fields of architecture, art, and culture. Our goal is to transform the Žižkov Tunnel into a vibrant gallery of contemporary art and an open public space.

The project connects the districts of Žižkov, Karlín, and Holešovice, forming a cultural axis through the heart of Prague. We draw inspiration from world-renowned projects such as the High Line in New York City.

The association is built on principles of openness, collaboration, and long-term care for public space.

list.name: Martin Wichterle
list.role: Industrialist, owner of the Wikov engineering group and the Bomma and Rückl glassworks

list.name: Jiří Řezák
list.role: Architect and owner of the QARTA Architektura studio

list.name: Jiří Fajt
list.role: Art historian and artistic director, former Director General of the National Gallery in Prague

history.title: History
history.intro: The Žižkov Tunnel is a unique piece of infrastructure connecting three Prague districts beneath Vítkov Hill. Today it serves as a daily corridor for pedestrians and cyclists — and we believe it can also serve as a passage into the world of culture.

timeline.text: The tunnel was built as part of the city's transport infrastructure and continues to serve that purpose to this day.
The Žižkov Tunnel Association is founded. A group of visionaries decides to transform the tunnel into a world-class gallery.
A ten-year plan envisions three installations per year by Czech and international artists. Each work is designed specifically for the tunnel space.
The first three artworks are installed. The tunnel opens to the public as a living gallery — the opening chapter of a cultural transformation.
A second wave of three installations broadens the thematic scope and brings in international artists. The tunnel establishes itself as a recognised cultural destination on the map of Prague.
The third phase closes the first decade with nine permanent works. The tunnel becomes an iconic site at the intersection of public space and contemporary art.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Documentation of the space and its transformations

findUs.title: How to Find Us
findUs.address:
Žižkov Tunnel
Prague 3 — Žižkov / Karlín / Holešovice
findUs.directions:
Entrance from Žižkov: Seifertova street, near Náměstí Winstona Churchilla.
Entrance from Karlín: Šaldova street.
Public transport: Metro B Florenc, tram Tachovské náměstí or Lipanská.
findUs.mapNote: The tunnel is open daily to pedestrians and cyclists.

meta.title: Žižkov Tunnel — Association
meta.description: Transforming the Žižkov Tunnel into a world-class art gallery. A project connecting Žižkov, Karlín and Holešovice — the cultural axis of Prague.
